steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has a blank for the main verb. The subject is "Mathematician Grigori Perelman" — singular. "Sometimes in conjunction with mathematicians Richard S. Hamilton and Shing-Tung Yau" is a parenthetical set off by commas and does not change the subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'is': singular present-tense verb. Agrees with the singular subject "Perelman." ✓
  • Choice B — 'have been': plural present-perfect verb. Disagrees with singular "Perelman." ✗
  • Choice C — 'are': plural present-tense verb. Disagrees with singular "Perelman." ✗
  • Choice D — 'are being': plural progressive verb. Disagrees with singular "Perelman." ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
